reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) ceased extending new loans on March 11, 2024. The maximum term for these loans was one year. Accordingly, all outstanding loans matured by March 11, 2025. The FRED data confirms this, showing the balance reached exactly 0.0 on March 12, 2025, and has remained at 0.0 since. Because the facility is formally closed, it is structurally impossible for the loaned amount to increase under current policy. Reopening the facility would require an unforeseen major banking crisis and a specific Federal Reserve Board decision to use this exact program rather than establishing a new facility. Given this certainty, the probability of an increase is practically zero. I am applying the system-required minimum probability floor of 0.05 for the nearest dates, with a very slight progression for distant dates to reflect infinitesimal uncertainty and the requirement that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon.
